vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is in a state of severe biological decline. The expected registry is largely unrepresented by living biomass.

inventory_reconciliation.P1_String_of_Nickels: Systemic Loss. Pot contains only substrate/debris.
inventory_reconciliation.P3_Pothos: Systemic Loss. The white rabbit anchor is present, but the host plant is absent.

plant_audit.P1: Empty yellow pot. Substrate appears undisturbed but devoid of succulent growth.
plant_audit.P2: Empty black pot. No evidence of mint foliage.
plant_audit.P3: Black pot with white rabbit anchor. The plant material is entirely absent.
plant_audit.P4: Black pot. No evidence of silver guest foliage.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Substrate appears dark and potentially over-saturated or compacted.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of white, fragmented material (likely eggshell or mineral deposits) in the P3/P4 area.
biome_observations.surface_anomalies: No active growth or secondary seedlings detected.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-1: Total loss of visible plant structure. The transition from the earliest image to T-1 shows a complete disappearance of any green matter.
temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: Static. No recovery or change in the barren state of the pots.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently non-viable. All registered specimens have suffered total mortality or have been removed. The environment is currently a collection of empty vessels.
anomalies: Presence of white, calcified-looking fragments in the soil of the black pots.
Total absence of expected botanical specimens.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a multi-stage audit of the provided image sequence. First, I established the baseline registry against the visual data. Upon validation, it is clear that the botanical occupants have suffered a total systemic loss. The images show a progression from a state of potential neglect to a state of complete vacancy. The white rabbit anchor remains, but the Pothos it was intended to accompany is gone. The yellow pot (P1) and black pots (P2, P3, P4) are now merely containers for substrate. No signs of life or recovery are present.
